title: Der Neuen Gedichte: Anderer Teil
summaries: "Der Neuen Gedichte: Anderer Teil" by Rainer Maria Rilke is a poetry collection published in 1908. This second volume represents Rilke's shift from emotional, subjective expression toward objective "thing-poems" inspired by visual art. Influenced by sculptor Auguste Rodin and painter Paul Cézanne, Rilke develops a new poetic language focused on seeing and observing the material world. These verses mark his transition to literary modernism, capturing physical reality while revealing deeper essence through precise, crafted observation. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
